preamble: The reduction of [u] or its short variant [ʊ] to [ə] is a common form of vowel reduction in stress-timed languages. In these systems, vowels in unstressed positions lose their distinct quality, rounding, and duration, centralizing toward the neutral schwa.

examples.note: Initial unstressed syllable reduces to schwa.
Weakening of function words in rapid speech.

In Central Atlas Tamazight, short /u/ in unstressed syllables may reduce to the central schwa [ə], paralleling similar reductions of /a/ and /i/. Tashlhiyt Berber goes further: proto-Berber short /u/ in certain environments corresponds to lexical schwa or zero in Tashlhiyt, while Tamazight retains a lexical schwa where Tashlhiyt allows consonant syllabification.

sources: Kossmann, M. G. & Stroomer, H. J. (1997). Berber Phonology. In Kaye (ed.), Phonologies of Asia and Africa. Eisenbrauns.
